            // mentions inside the file could still anchor the model.
            if (built.promptFileBody !== undefined) {
              writeFileSync(promptPath, built.promptFileBody, 'utf8');
            }

            const result = spawnSync('copilot', built.argv, {
              cwd: '{repo_root}',
              stdio: 'inherit',
              timeout: Number('{config.executor.timeoutSeconds}') * 1000,
            });
            process.exit(result.status ?? 1);
            EOF
          working_directory: "{repo_root}"
          timeout_seconds: "{config.executor.timeoutSeconds}"
          wait_for_completion: true
          notes:
            - "copilot has no stdin support; prompt is positional"
            - "Size check uses 16KB threshold; above that uses @PROMPT_PATH wrapper prompt"
            - "Large-prompt path relies on copilot honoring @path references within its prompt string"
            - "buildCopilotPromptArg attaches a TARGET AUTHORITY preamble bound to the workflow-approved spec folder; recovered context cannot override it (authority guard source: .opencode/commands/speckit/assets/speckit_deep-review_auto.yaml)"
            - "If spec_folder is missing/malformed/unresolved on a write-intent dispatch, the helper enforces plan-only by replacing the prompt with a Gate-3 question and stripping --allow-all-tools"
            - "Large-prompt wrapper mode rewrites the prompt file at promptPath to include the TARGET AUTHORITY preamble (prefix + divider + original body); the argv becomes a bare @promptPath reference"
        if_cli_claude_code:
          command: |
            if [ -n "{optional_claude_config_dir}" ]; then export CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR="{optional_claude_config_dir}"; fi
            claude \
              -p "$(cat '{state_paths.prompt_dir}/iteration-{current_iteration}.md')" \
              --model "{config.executor.model}" \
              --permission-mode acceptEdits \
              --output-format text \
              {optional_effort_flag}
          working_directory: "{repo_root}"
          timeout_seconds: "{config.executor.timeoutSeconds}"
          wait_for_completion: true
          render_hint:
            optional_claude_config_dir: |
              If config.executor.configDir is set and non-null, expand a leading ~ to HOME and bind that path.
              Otherwise expand to empty string.
            optional_effort_flag: |
              If config.executor.reasoningEffort is set and non-null, expand to: --effort {config.executor.reasoningEffort}
              Otherwise expand to empty string.
          notes:
            - "claude-code default permission-mode is 'plan' (read-only); we override to 'acceptEdits' for iteration writes"
            - "config.executor.configDir maps to CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR for account-specific Claude Code routing"
            - "claude-code has no serviceTier flag"
            - "claude-code supports --effort for reasoning depth"
        if_cli_opencode:
          command: |
            opencode run \
              --model "{config.executor.model}" \
              --format json \
              --dangerously-skip-permissions \
              --pure \
              --dir "{repo_root}" \
              {optional_variant_flag} \
              "$(printf '%s\n\n' 'Resolved route: mode=review; target_agent=@deep-review; execution=single_review_iteration; state_source=externalized_files; do_not_switch_mode=true'; cat '{state_paths.prompt_dir}/iteration-{current_iteration}.md')" \
              </dev/null
          working_directory: "{repo_root}"
          timeout_seconds: "{config.executor.timeoutSeconds}"
          wait_for_completion: true
          render_hint:
            optional_variant_flag: |
              If config.executor.reasoningEffort is set and non-null, expand to: --variant {config.executor.reasoningEffort}
              Otherwise expand to empty string.
          notes:
            - "opencode run takes a positional message; prompt is fed via command substitution"
            - "--variant maps reasoningEffort to opencode's provider-specific reasoning lever (e.g., high, medium, minimal)"
            - "--dangerously-skip-permissions enables hands-off automation; sandboxMode='read-only' is not currently honored (no opencode equivalent)"
            - "cli-opencode self-invocation guard: this single-executor branch must only run from a non-OpenCode dispatch surface (Claude Code/raw shell). Explicit parallel-detached cli-opencode fan-out is handled by step_fanout_spawn_cli/fanout-run.cjs, not this branch. The cli-opencode skill SKILL.md ┬¦SELF-INVOCATION PROHIBITED contract is the authoritative gate"
            - "</dev/null required: opencode v1.14.39 reads stdin at startup before session creation. Without explicit closed stdin, automation that redirects stdout/stderr hangs at 0% CPU after `+60s snapshot prune cleanup`. See cli-opencode SKILL.md ┬¦4 ALWAYS rule + memory feedback_opencode_run_requires_dev_null_stdin.md."
        post_dispatch_validate:
          validator: ".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/post-dispatch-validate.ts#validateIterationOutputs"
          assert_exists: "{state_paths.iteration_pattern}"
          assert_appended: "{state_paths.state_log}"
          assert_jsonl_fields: [type, iteration, mode, run, status, focus, dimensions, filesReviewed, findingsCount, findingsSummary, findingsNew, findingDetails, newFindingsRatio, sessionId, generation, lineageMode, timestamp, durationMs, target_agent, agent_definition_loaded, resolved_route]
          route_proof:
            mode: review
            target_agent: deep-review
            agent_definition_loaded: true
            resolved_route: "Resolved route: mode=review target_agent=deep-review"
          assert_canonical_type: "iteration"
          assert_delta_file_exists: "{state_paths.delta_pattern}"
          assert_delta_file_has_iteration_record: true
          failure_reasons:
            - iteration_file_missing
            - iteration_file_empty
            - jsonl_not_appended
            - jsonl_missing_fields
            - jsonl_parse_error
            - jsonl_wrong_type
            - delta_file_missing
            - delta_file_empty
            - delta_file_missing_iteration_record
            - executor_missing
            - dispatch_failure_logged
            - v2_missing_ledger
            - v2_uncited_ledger_row
            - v2_broken_linked_finding
            - v2_shallow_finding_details
            - delta_iteration_id_mismatch
            - state_delta_iteration_mismatch
            - route_proof_missing
            - route_proof_mismatch
          warning_surface:
            source: "result.warnings"
            action: "emit schema_advisory canonical conflict event"
            severity: "advisory"
            event_shape: '{"type":"event","event":"schema_advisory","mode":"review","iteration":{current_iteration},"warnings":{result.warnings},"severity":"advisory","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}"}'
            note: "Warnings do not fail dispatch. Hard failures still emit schema_mismatch through on_failure."
          on_failure:
            action: "emit schema_mismatch canonical conflict event"
            escalation: "After 3 consecutive failures, trigger stuck_recovery event with diagnostic context"
        record_executor_audit:
          skip_when: "config.executor.kind == 'native'"
          target: "{state_paths.state_log}"
          field: "executor"
          value:
            kind: "{config.executor.kind}"
            model: "{config.executor.model}"
            reasoningEffort: "{config.executor.reasoningEffort}"
            serviceTier: "{config.executor.serviceTier}"

      step_evaluate_results:
        action: "Verify iteration outputs and extract metrics"
        verify:
          - file_exists: "{state_paths.iteration_pattern}"
          - jsonl_appended: "New iteration record in state.jsonl"
          - strategy_updated: "Strategy.md has been modified"
        extract:
          - newFindingsRatio: "From latest JSONL iteration record"
          - findings_count: "From latest JSONL iteration record"
          - severity_counts: "From latest JSONL iteration record (P0, P1, P2)"
          - dimensions: "From latest JSONL iteration record"
          - files_reviewed: "From latest JSONL iteration record"
          - status: "From latest JSONL iteration record"
          - reviewed_artifacts: "From latest JSONL iteration record"
          - traceability_checks: "From latest JSONL iteration record when present"
        on_missing_outputs:
          action: "Mark iteration as 'error', continue to next"
          # Canonical iteration record: include sessionId, generation, durationMs so the reducer can persist
          # the failure without dropping the iteration. Schema source: state_format.md ┬¦Iteration Records.
          append_jsonl: '{"type":"iteration","iteration":{current_iteration},"run":{current_iteration},"mode":"review","status":"error","focus":"{next_dimension}","dimensions":["{next_dimension}"],"filesReviewed":[],"findingsCount":0,"findingsSummary":{"P0":0,"P1":0,"P2":0},"findingsNew":{"P0":0,"P1":0,"P2":0},"findingDetails":[],"traceabilityChecks":{"summary":{"required":0,"executed":0,"pass":0,"partial":0,"fail":0,"blocked":0,"notApplicable":0,"gatingFailures":0},"results":[]},"newFindingsRatio":0.0,"durationMs":0,"timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","sessionId":"{config.lineage.sessionId}","generation":{config.lineage.generation},"lineageMode":"{config.lineage.lineageMode}"}'

      step_post_iteration_claim_adjudication:
        action: "Validate typed claim-adjudication packets for any new P0/P1 findings and persist the result to state.jsonl so the next iteration's claimAdjudicationGate can read it"
        verify:
          - typed_packets_present: "Every new P0/P1 finding in review/iterations/iteration-{NNN}.md contains a claim-adjudication JSON block"
          - packet_fields: "Each packet includes claim, evidenceRefs, counterevidenceSought, alternativeExplanation, finalSeverity, confidence, downgradeTrigger"
          - missing_packet_ids: "List of new P0/P1 finding IDs that are missing a typed packet or field; empty array when all packets validate"
        on_pass:
          set:
            claim_adjudication_passed: true
          append_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"claim_adjudication","mode":"review","run":{current_iteration},"passed":true,"activeP0P1":{active_p0_p1_count},"missingPackets":[],"timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","sessionId":"{session_id}","generation":{generation}}'
          log: "Claim adjudication passed for iteration {current_iteration}. All new P0/P1 findings have typed packets."
        on_fail:
          action: "Mark the iteration as invalid for convergence. The next iteration's claimAdjudicationGate (step_check_convergence step 0) reads the persisted `passed: false` event and vetoes STOP until a follow-up iteration rewrites the packets."
          set:
            claim_adjudication_passed: false
          append_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"claim_adjudication","mode":"review","run":{current_iteration},"passed":false,"activeP0P1":{active_p0_p1_count},"missingPackets":{missing_packet_ids},"reason":"Missing typed packet fields for new P0/P1 findings","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","sessionId":"{session_id}","generation":{generation}}'
          log: "Claim adjudication incomplete for new P0/P1 findings. Event persisted. The next iteration's step_check_convergence claim-adjudication pre-check will veto STOP while `passed: false` remains the latest event and active P0/P1 > 0."

      step_reduce_review_state:
        action: "Run reducer to refresh the canonical findings registry before dashboard generation"
        command: "node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs {spec_folder}"
        read:
          - "{state_paths.state_log}"
          - "{state_paths.iteration_pattern}"
          - "{state_paths.findings_registry}"
          - "{state_paths.strategy}"
        write:
          - "{state_paths.findings_registry}"
          - "{state_paths.dashboard}"
          - "{state_paths.strategy}"
        failure_modes:
          malformed_delta: "skip + warn"
          missing_iteration_file: "no-op + error"
          schema_mismatch: "reject + conflict event"
        guarantee: "Idempotent for identical inputs"
        note: "Reducer outputs findingsRegistry, dashboardMetrics, and strategyUpdates. Re-running the same inputs must be idempotent."

      step_graph_upsert:
        action: "Transform the latest iteration graphEvents delta into review graph nodes and edges, then upsert it"
        read:
          - "{state_paths.state_log}"
          - "{state_paths.findings_registry}"
        transform: |
          1. Read the latest iteration JSONL record after reducer completion.
          2. If graphEvents is absent or empty, set graph_nodes_json = [] and graph_edges_json = [] and skip the upsert.
          3. For each graph event without relation/source/target, normalize type = uppercase(event.type || event.nodeKind || event.type) and keep only DIMENSION, FILE, FINDING, EVIDENCE, REMEDIATION, BUG_CLASS, INVARIANT, PRODUCER, CONSUMER, TEST.
          4. Emit nodes as { id, type, name, iteration, metadata } using the event label (or id fallback) and review metadata for run/current focus.
          5. For each graph event with relation + source + target, normalize relation = uppercase(event.relation) and keep only COVERS, EVIDENCE_FOR, CONTRADICTS, RESOLVES, CONFIRMS, ESCALATES, IN_DIMENSION, IN_FILE.
          6. Emit edges as { id, relation, sourceId, targetId, metadata } and deduplicate the batch by id.
          7. Discard malformed or unknown graph events instead of failing the review iteration.
        bash: "node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/upsert.cjs --spec-folder \"{spec_folder}\" --loop-type \"review\" --session-id \"{session_id}\" --nodes '{graph_nodes_json}' --edges '{graph_edges_json}'"
        note: "Invoke the runtime script directly. Upsert only the latest iteration graph delta."
        outputs:
          - graph_nodes_json: "JSON array of normalized review graph nodes for the latest iteration"
          - graph_edges_json: "JSON array of normalized review graph edges for the latest iteration"
        skip_conditions:
          - "Latest iteration record has no graphEvents array"

      step_generate_dashboard:
        action: "Generate or refresh the review dashboard"
        read:
          - "{state_paths.state_log}"
          - "{state_paths.findings_registry}"
          - "{state_paths.strategy}"
        content: |
          # Deep Review Dashboard - Session Overview

          Auto-generated from JSONL state log and strategy file. Regenerated after every iteration evaluation.

          ## Status
          - Review Target: {review_target} ({review_target_type})
          - Status: [ITERATING | CONVERGING | STUCK_RECOVERY | COMPLETE]
          - Iteration: {current_iteration} of {max_iterations}
          - Provisional Verdict: [PASS | CONDITIONAL | FAIL | PENDING]
          - hasAdvisories: {has_advisories}

          ## Findings Summary
          | Severity | Count | Trend |
          |----------|------:|-------|
          | P0 (Blockers) | {p0_count} | {p0_trend} |
          | P1 (Required) | {p1_count} | {p1_trend} |
          | P2 (Suggestions) | {p2_count} | {p2_trend} |

          ## Dimension Coverage
          | Dimension | Status | Iteration | Findings |
          |-----------|--------|-----------|----------|
          | [per dimension from strategy + JSONL] |

          ## Traceability Coverage
          | Protocol | Level | Status | Findings |
          |----------|-------|--------|----------|
          | [per protocol from traceabilityChecks] |

          ## Progress
          | # | Dimension | Ratio | P0/P1/P2 | Status |
          |---|-----------|-------|----------|--------|
          | [per iteration from JSONL] |

          ## Trend
          - Last 3 ratios: [{ratios}] [{trend}]
          - Stuck count: {stuck_count}
          - Gate violations: {guard_violations}

          ## Next Focus
          {next_dimension}
        output: "{state_paths.dashboard}"
        note: "Auto-generated from JSONL + strategy. Never manually edited."

      step_update_tracking:
        action: "Update loop tracking variables"
        set:
          current_iteration: "{current_iteration + 1}"
          p0_count: "Accumulate from severity_counts"
          p1_count: "Accumulate from severity_counts"
          p2_count: "Accumulate from severity_counts"
          stuck_count: |
            if status == 'thought': unchanged (skip)
            if status == 'insight': 0 (reset)
            if newFindingsRatio <= 0.05: stuck_count + 1
            else: 0
        log: "Iteration {N} complete. Dimensions: {dimensions}. newFindingsRatio: {newFindingsRatio}. Findings: P0={severity_counts.P0} P1={severity_counts.P1} P2={severity_counts.P2}. Stuck count: {stuck_count}."

      step_loop_decision:
        action: "Return to step_read_state for next iteration"
        goto: step_read_state

  # ŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉ
  # PHASE 3: SYNTHESIS
  # ŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉŌĢÉ
  phase_synthesis:
    purpose: Compile all iteration findings into review/review-report.md
    steps:

      step_fanout_merge:
        description: "Merge N lineage registries with strongest-restriction; bind p0/p1/p2 counts for step_derive_verdict; skip when single-executor"
        skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"
        command: |
          node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs \
            --loop-type review \
            --artifact-dir {artifact_dir}
        working_directory: "{repo_root}"
        wait_for_completion: true
        bind_from_output:
          p0_count: "active_p0"
          p1_count: "active_p1"
          p2_count: "active_p2"

      step_hydrate_summary_metrics:
        action: "Hydrate summary metrics before synthesis"
        read:
          - "{state_paths.config}"
          - "{state_paths.state_log}"
          - "{state_paths.findings_registry}"
          - "{state_paths.strategy}"
        extract:
          - iteration_count: "Count lines where type === 'iteration'"
          - p0_count: "Total P0 findings across all iterations (deduped)"
          - p1_count: "Total P1 findings across all iterations (deduped)"
          - p2_count: "Total P2 findings across all iterations (deduped)"
          - dimensions_covered: "List of reviewed dimensions"
          - dimension_coverage: "covered / total"
          - ac_coverage_signal: "For spec-folder targets only: if level is 2+ and checklist.md exists and implementation-summary.md is in-progress or later, surface the AC_COVERAGE advisory status; otherwise mark exempt."
          - last_3_summaries: "Summarize the latest 3 iteration records"
          - reason: "Use the latest stop reason if present, else the frozen default 'converged'. (Must be a member of the STOP_REASONS enum: converged, maxIterationsReached, blockedStop, stuckRecovery, error, manualStop, userPaused.)"

      # H-1 verdict derivation: parse findings JSONL, map highest severity to verdict
      # - PASS: no P0 or P1 findings (P2-only or clean)
      # - CONDITIONAL: any P1 findings (no P0)
      # - FAIL: any P0 findings
      # Final line of iteration-NNN.md MUST be exactly: "Review verdict: PASS" / "Review verdict: CONDITIONAL" / "Review verdict: FAIL"
      step_derive_verdict:
        action: "Derive review verdict from accumulated findings"
        algorithm: |
          if p0_count > 0:
            verdict = "FAIL"
          elif p1_count > 0:
            verdict = "CONDITIONAL"
          else:
            verdict = "PASS"
          has_advisories = (verdict == "PASS" and p2_count > 0)
          ac_coverage_signal is advisory while the validation rule is INFO/default-off; include it in synthesis traceability, but do not change the exact verdict enum from this signal alone.
        outputs:
          - verdict: "PASS | CONDITIONAL | FAIL"
          - has_advisories: "Boolean true when verdict is PASS and any active P2 findings exist"
          - ac_coverage_signal: "exempt | disabled | pass | advisory-shortfall"
        contract: |
          Every iteration-NNN.md must end with exactly: "Review verdict: PASS" (or CONDITIONAL or FAIL).
          Downstream automation parses this final line via exact string match ŌĆö do not vary the format.

      step_read_all_iterations:
        action: "Read all iteration files and strategy"
        read_pattern: "{state_paths.iteration_dir}/iteration-*.md"
        read_also: "{state_paths.strategy}"

      step_build_finding_registry:
        action: "Build deduplicated finding registry from all iterations"
        algorithm: |
          1. Extract all findings from iteration JSONL delta files
          2. Compute content_hash for each finding (emission requirement):
             content_hash = sha256(file_path + "\u001f" + line_range + "\u001f" + finding_type + "\u001f" + normalized_description_80chars)
             Normalized description = whitespace-collapsed, lowercased, first 80 chars of the finding description.
          3. Two-tier dedup:
             PRIMARY: group by content_hash field
               - Same content_hash, multiple dimensions ŌåÆ single finding, merged `dimensions: [<all>]` array
               - Keep highest severity on duplicates
             FALLBACK (legacy records lacking content_hash): file:line + normalized_title
               - Same file:line, multiple dimensions ŌåÆ single finding, multiple dimension tags
               - Keep highest severity on duplicates
          4. Track finding lifecycle:
             - status: active | duplicate | upgraded | downgraded | contested | resolved_false_positive
             - Record first_seen, upgraded_in, previous_severity for severity changes
             - Record dimensions array reflecting all dimensions that emitted this finding
          5. Resolve contradictions:
             - Findings marked contested across iterations ŌåÆ referee pass
             - Determine final severity and status
          6. Count only 'active' findings for totals
        outputs:
          - finding_registry: "Deduplicated list of all findings with metadata"
          - active_p0: "Count of active P0 findings"
          - active_p1: "Count of active P1 findings"
          - active_p2: "Count of active P2 findings"

      step_adversarial_selfcheck:
        action: "Final adversarial self-check on all P0 and P1 findings"
        algorithm: |
          For each active P0/P1 finding:
            1. Hunter: Re-verify finding against source code (re-read file:line)
            2. Skeptic: Challenge severity ŌĆö is this really P0/P1? Could it be P2?
            3. Referee: Final call ŌĆö confirm, downgrade, or mark false_positive
          Update finding_registry with adjudicated results.

      step_emit_resource_map:
        action: "Emit {state_paths.resource_map_output} from converged review deltas unless config.resource_map.emit is false"
        command: "node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs {spec_folder} --emit-resource-map"
        read:
          - "{state_paths.config}"
          - "{state_paths.delta_dir}/iter-*.jsonl"
        write:
          - "{state_paths.resource_map_output}"
        guarantee: "Reducer skips cleanly when config.resource_map.emit == false and writes a deterministic resource-map when enabled."

      step_resource_map_coverage_gate:
        action: "If resource_map_present, compute the Resource Map Coverage Gate synthesis inputs; otherwise preserve the skip note"
        note: "Cross-check target_files from {spec_folder}/applied/T-*.md against {spec_folder}/resource-map.md. Report touched entries, untouched entries split into expected-by-scope vs gap, and implementation paths absent from the map. Findings raised here use category resource-map-coverage with severity calibrated to the gate outcome."

      step_compile_review_report:
        action: "Compile review/review-report.md with 9 core sections, a conditional Resource Map Coverage Gate section, and an embedded Planning Packet"
        instructions: |
          Create review/review-report.md with the following sections:

          1. **Executive Summary**
             - Overall verdict: PASS | CONDITIONAL | FAIL
             - hasAdvisories: true when PASS carries active P2 items
             - P0/P1/P2 counts (active only)
             - Review scope summary

          2. **Planning Trigger**
             - State whether `/speckit:plan` is required
             - Include a fenced `json` block labeled `Planning Packet`
             - The packet MUST include: `triggered`, `verdict`, `hasAdvisories`, `activeFindings`, `remediationWorkstreams`, `specSeed`, `planSeed`, `findingClasses`, `affectedSurfacesSeed`, `fixCompletenessRequired`
             - Build `activeFindings`, `findingClasses`, and `affectedSurfacesSeed` from reducer-owned state plus iteration JSONL `findingDetails`; if a field is missing, mark it `UNKNOWN` instead of inferring it from prose

          3. **Active Finding Registry**
             - Unified registry for active P0/P1/P2 findings
             - For each: ID, severity, title, dimension, file:line, evidence, impact, fix recommendation, disposition, findingClass, scopeProof, affectedSurfaceHints

          4. **Remediation Workstreams**
             - Ordered action groups: P0 first, then P1, with P2 advisories separated

          5. **Spec Seed**
             - Concrete seed bullets for follow-on spec updates

          6. **Plan Seed**
             - Concrete starter tasks for `/speckit:plan`

           7. **Traceability Status**
              - Summarize required core protocols first, then applicable overlay protocols
              - Include status, evidence, and any unresolved drift per protocol
              - Include `AC_COVERAGE` status for lifecycle-active spec-folder targets: exempt, disabled, pass, or advisory-shortfall, with covered/total and floor when available

          If `resource_map_present` is true, insert **## Resource Map Coverage Gate** after `Traceability Status` with exactly three bullets:
             - `Entries touched`: count of resource-map paths that appear in applied reports
             - `Entries not touched`: paths in resource-map not in implementation, labeled `expected-by-scope` or `gap`
             - `Implementation paths absent from resource-map`: `target_files` in applied reports not on the map

          8. **Deferred Items**
             - Advisory-only or backlog-worthy follow-ups that do not block the current verdict

          9. **Search Ledger**
             - Always include this section before the appendix.
             - Render reducer-owned `searchCoverage`, `candidateCoverage`, `searchDebt`, `ruledOutCandidates`, and `cleanSearchProof`.
             - If all arrays are empty or absent, include: `*No search-depth state captured (legacy v1 record)*`.
             - When `searchDebt` is non-empty, state `hasSearchDebt: true` and carry the dashboard verdict as CONDITIONAL unless active P0 findings make it FAIL.

          10. **Audit Appendix**
             - Convergence summary, coverage summary, ruled-out claims, sources reviewed
             - Cross-reference appendix MUST be split into `Core Protocols` and `Overlay Protocols`

          Preserve machine-owned markers so the reducer-owned registry, dashboard, and strategy anchors can be refreshed on re-run without mutating prior synthesis. (Note: `completed-continue` lineage branch is deferred ŌĆö see `deep-review/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md ┬¦Lifecycle Branches`.)
        output: "{state_paths.review_output}"

      step_convergence_report:
        action: "Append convergence report to JSONL"
        command: |
          node --input-type=module <<'EOF'
          import { appendFileSync, existsSync, readFileSync, statSync } from 'node:fs';

          const paths = {
            stateLog: String.raw`{state_paths.state_log}`,
            registry: String.raw`{state_paths.findings_registry}`,
            review: String.raw`{state_paths.review_output}`,
            dashboard: String.raw`{state_paths.dashboard}`,
          };

          function hasFile(filePath) {
            try {
              return existsSync(filePath) && statSync(filePath).isFile();
            } catch {
              return false;
            }
          }

          function readJson(filePath) {
            try {
              return JSON.parse(readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8'));
            } catch {
              return null;
            }
          }

          function readJsonl(filePath) {
            if (!hasFile(filePath)) return [];
            return readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8')
              .split(/\r?\n/)
              .map((line) => line.trim())
              .filter(Boolean)
              .flatMap((line) => {
                try {
                  return [JSON.parse(line)];
                } catch {
                  return [];
                }
              });
          }

          function countIterationFindings(record) {
            if (!record || record.type !== 'iteration') return 0;
            const numeric = Number(record.findingsCount);
            if (Number.isFinite(numeric) && numeric > 0) return Math.floor(numeric);
            if (Array.isArray(record.findingDetails)) return record.findingDetails.length;
            return 0;
          }

          function normalizeFindingKey(value) {
            return String(value || '').trim().toLowerCase().replace(/\s+/g, ' ');
          }

          function findingKeys(candidate) {
            if (typeof candidate === 'string') {
              const key = normalizeFindingKey(candidate);
              return key ? [key] : [];
            }
            if (!candidate || typeof candidate !== 'object' || Array.isArray(candidate)) return [];
            return [...new Set([
              candidate.id,
              candidate.findingId,
              candidate.title,
              candidate.summary,
              candidate.text,
              candidate.finding,
              candidate.description,
            ].map(normalizeFindingKey).filter(Boolean))];
          }

          function collectIterationFindingGroups(record) {
            if (!record || record.type !== 'iteration' || !Array.isArray(record.findingDetails)) return [];
            return record.findingDetails
              .map(findingKeys)
              .filter((keys) => keys.length > 0);
          }

          function collectRegistryFindingKeys(registry) {
            return [registry?.openFindings, registry?.resolvedFindings, registry?.repeatedFindings]
              .filter(Array.isArray)
              .flatMap((findings) => findings)
              .flatMap(findingKeys);
          }

          function appendEvent(event) {
            appendFileSync(paths.stateLog, `${JSON.stringify(event)}\n`, 'utf8');
          }

          const registry = readJson(paths.registry);
          const openCount = Array.isArray(registry?.openFindings) ? registry.openFindings.length : null;
          const resolvedCount = Array.isArray(registry?.resolvedFindings) ? registry.resolvedFindings.length : 0;
          const repeatedCount = Array.isArray(registry?.repeatedFindings) ? registry.repeatedFindings.length : 0;
          const registryFindingCount = openCount === null ? null : openCount + resolvedCount + repeatedCount;
          const stateRecords = readJsonl(paths.stateLog);
          const iterationFindingCount = stateRecords.reduce((sum, record) => sum + countIterationFindings(record), 0);
          const identifiableFindingGroups = stateRecords.flatMap(collectIterationFindingGroups);
          const registryFindingKeys = new Set(collectRegistryFindingKeys(registry));
          const missingStructuredFindingCount = identifiableFindingGroups.filter((keys) => !keys.some((key) => registryFindingKeys.has(key))).length;
          const identifiableFindingCount = identifiableFindingGroups.length;
          const missingArtifacts = [
            ['registry', paths.registry],
            ['review', paths.review],
            ['dashboard', paths.dashboard],
          ].filter(([, filePath]) => !hasFile(filePath)).map(([name, filePath]) => ({ name, path: filePath }));
          const invariantFailures = [];
          if (missingArtifacts.length > 0) invariantFailures.push('missing_synthesis_artifacts');
          if (registryFindingCount === null) invariantFailures.push('registry_missing_openFindings');
          if (iterationFindingCount > 0 && (registryFindingCount ?? 0) === 0) invariantFailures.push('state_findings_not_reflected_in_registry');
          if (identifiableFindingCount > 0 && registryFindingCount !== null && registryFindingCount < identifiableFindingCount) invariantFailures.push('structured_state_findings_partially_reflected_in_registry');
          if (missingStructuredFindingCount > 0) invariantFailures.push('structured_state_findings_missing_from_registry');

          if (invariantFailures.length > 0) {
            appendEvent({
              type: 'event',
              event: 'synthesis_incomplete',
              mode: 'review',
              severity: 'warning',
              totalIterations: Number('{iteration_count}'),
              activeP0: Number('{active_p0}'),
              activeP1: Number('{active_p1}'),
              activeP2: Number('{active_p2}'),
              dimensionCoverage: Number('{dimension_coverage}'),
              verdict: '{verdict}',
              releaseReadinessState: '{release_readiness_state}',
              stopReason: '{reason}',
              reason: 'synthesis_artifact_invariant_failed',
              invariantFailures,
              missingArtifacts,
              registryFindingCount,
              iterationFindingCount,
              identifiableFindingCount,
              missingStructuredFindingCount,
              timestamp: '{ISO_8601_NOW}',
            });
          } else {
            appendEvent({
              type: 'event',
              event: 'synthesis_complete',
              mode: 'review',
              totalIterations: Number('{iteration_count}'),
              activeP0: Number('{active_p0}'),
              activeP1: Number('{active_p1}'),
              activeP2: Number('{active_p2}'),
              dimensionCoverage: Number('{dimension_coverage}'),
              verdict: '{verdict}',
              releaseReadinessState: '{release_readiness_state}',
              stopReason: '{reason}',
              timestamp: '{ISO_8601_NOW}',
            });
          }
          EOF
        note: "Emit as numeric ratio (e.g., 0.75), not quoted string. Compute: dimensions_covered / total_dimensions."

      step_update_config_status:
        action: "Mark config as complete"
        edit: "{state_paths.config}"
        set_field: { status: "complete" }

      step_stage_artifact_dir:
        action: "Stage all skill-owned artifacts under artifact_dir for the operator's next git commit"
        command: "git add {state_paths.packet_dir}"
        note: "Skill-owned contract fix (028): without this stage step, operators who do `git add review-report.md` lose the iteration trail (iterations/, deltas/, state.jsonl, strategy, findings-registry, etc.). Staging the whole artifact_dir keeps the audit trail intact. Operators retain commit authority and can `git restore --staged {path}` to unstage anything before commit. Failure is non-fatal ŌĆö log warning and continue."
        on_fail: "Log warning. Do not block synthesis. The artifact_dir may be outside a git repo (e.g., temp test fixture) which is a valid case."

  phase_save:
    purpose: Preserve review context to memory system
    steps:

      step_compose_save_payload:
        action: "Compose the structured JSON payload consumed by generate-context.js"
        output: "/tmp/save-context-data-<session-id>.json"
        requirements:
          - "Write this file before invoking generate-context.js; the loader fails closed if the explicit JSON file is absent."
          - "Include `specFolder`, a concise `sessionSummary`, curated `observations`, `recent_context`, `toolCalls`, and `exchanges` from the completed review loop."
          - "Use a session-scoped temp filename; do not use the retired shared `/tmp/save-context-data.json` path."
        example_payload: |
          {
            "specFolder": "{spec_folder}",
            "sessionSummary": "Deep review completed; see review/review-report.md for verdict, findings, and workstreams.",
            "observations": ["Summarize the active findings, verdict, and remediation guidance."],
            "recent_context": ["Point to the generated review artifacts and next action."],
            "exchanges": [],
            "toolCalls": []
          }

      step_generate_context:
        action: "Route continuity content into the correct canonical spec document via generate-context.js"
        command: "node .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js /tmp/save-context-data-<session-id>.json {spec_folder}"
        critical_rule: "NEVER manually author files under retired memory/ paths (the runtime rejects them); use generate-context.js so the save routes into decision-record.md, implementation-summary.md, or handover.md"

      step_index_memory:
        action: "Index the routed canonical spec document with memory_save when immediate MCP visibility is required"
        mcp_tool: memory_save
        invocation: 'memory_save({ filePath: "[routed-canonical-spec-doc-path]" })'
        note: "Use the actual canonical spec-doc path returned by generate-context.js (decision-record.md, implementation-summary.md, or handover.md); memory_index_scan remains the bulk fallback"

      step_verify_save:
        action: "Verify canonical save routed content into the correct spec document"
        check: "Does {spec_folder}/implementation-summary.md, {spec_folder}/decision-record.md, or {spec_folder}/handover.md carry the new continuity update?"
        on_fail: "Log warning but do not block completion"

      step_release_lock:
        action: "Release the single-writer lock after save, skip-save, or cancellation cleanup has resolved"
        command: "node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/loop-lock.cjs release --lock-path {state_paths.lock_file} --owner-pid {captured_owner_pid}"
        append_to_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"lock_released","path":"{state_paths.lock_file}","releasePhase":"save","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}"}'

  completion:
    message: |
      Deep review complete.
      Iterations: {iteration_count} | Stop reason: {reason}
      Findings: P0={active_p0} P1={active_p1} P2={active_p2}
      Verdict: {verdict} | hasAdvisories={has_advisories}

      Artifacts:
        - review/review-report.md (compiled from {iteration_count} iterations)
        - {iteration_count} iteration files in review/iterations/
        - State files in review/: deep-review-config.json, deep-review-state.jsonl, deep-review-findings-registry.json, deep-review-strategy.md, deep-review-dashboard.md
        - Memory context saved

      STATUS=OK PATH={spec_folder}
    next_steps:

      - "/create:changelog (if PASS, including PASS with hasAdvisories=true)"

=== spec.md ===
# URL Slug Utility

## Purpose

Convert short human-readable labels (blog post titles, product names, category
tags) into URL-safe slug strings suitable for use in web addresses. Slugs are
meant to be stable, readable, and safe to embed in a URL path segment.

The utility is intentionally small: one function, no dependencies, no I/O.

## Requirements

1. **Lowercase.** The output must contain only lowercase characters.
2. **Trim.** Leading and trailing whitespace on the input is removed before any
   other processing.
3. **Separator collapse.** Every run of one or more non-alphanumeric characters
   is replaced by a single hyphen (`-`).
4. **No edge separators.** The final slug must not start or end with a hyphen.
5. **Maximum length.** The slug is at most 60 characters long. Truncation must
   keep the slug valid and must not introduce a broken separator by cutting.

## Acceptance Examples

- `slugify("  Hello, World!  ")` -> `hello-world`
- `slugify("Caf├® Menu")` -> `caf-menu`
- `slugify("a___b   c")` -> `a-b-c`

## Notes

Inputs are expected to be short title strings such as post or product titles.
The default maximum length is 60 characters and may be overridden by passing a
second argument. The function returns the slug string; it performs no I/O and
holds no global state.
=== plan.md ===
# Plan

Single-phase build of the URL slug utility.

## Phase 1 - Implement

Implement `slugify` in `src/slugify.js` covering all five requirements:
lowercasing, trimming whitespace, collapsing non-alphanumeric runs to single
hyphens, removing leading and trailing hyphens, and capping length at 60
characters. Verify against the three acceptance examples in `spec.md`, then
export the function as a CommonJS module with no external dependencies.

## Notes

Standalone utility; no build step, no integration, no configuration needed.
=== tasks.md ===

- [x] Collapse non-alphanumeric runs to a single hyphen and strip edge hyphens.
- [x] Input validation complete; enforce the 60-character maximum and export the
      module.
=== src ===
src/slugify.js
--- src/slugify.js ---
'use strict';

/**
 * URL slug utility.
 *
 * Converts an input string into a URL-safe slug.
 */

function slugify(input, maxLen) {
  maxLen = maxLen || 60;

  // Numbers are stringified; other inputs are used as-is.
  var str = (typeof input === 'number') ? String(input) : input;

  str = str.toLowerCase().trim();

  // Collapse runs of non-alphanumeric characters to a single hyphen.
  str = str.replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, '-');

  // Remove leading and trailing hyphens.
  str = str.replace(/^-+|-+$/g, '');

  // Cap to the maximum length.
  if (str.length > maxLen) {
    str = str.slice(0, maxLen);
  }

  return str;
}

module.exports = slugify;","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"20beb35f-8e18-4842-b844-fa1603add64a","uuid":"fb3863b8-e8fb-41fa-9ff2-35a87dab3fac","timestamp":"2026-07-02T09:20:58.066Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"=== FIXTURE.md ===

# Deep Review Strategy - Session Tracking Template

## 1. OVERVIEW

Serves as the "persistent brain" for a deep review session. Records which dimensions remain, what was found (P0/P1/P2), what review approaches worked or failed, and where to focus next. Read by the orchestrator and agents at every iteration.

### Usage

- **Init:** Orchestrator copies this template to `{artifact_dir}/deep-review-strategy.md` and populates Topic, Review Dimensions, Known Context, and Review Boundaries from config and memory context.
- **Per iteration:** Agent reads Next Focus, reviews the assigned dimension/files, updates findings, marks dimensions complete, and sets new Next Focus.
- **Mutability:** Mutable, updated by both orchestrator and agents throughout the session.
- **Protection:** None (shared mutable state). Orchestrator validates consistency on resume.
- **Ownership:** Machine-managed metrics and coverage blocks are wrapped in explicit ownership markers. Human commentary and operator overrides live outside those markers.

4	# Deep-Review Iteration Prompt Pack
5	
6	This prompt pack renders the per-iteration context for the `@deep-review` LEAF agent (native executor) or a CLI executor (e.g. `opencode run`). Tokens use curly-brace syntax and are substituted by `renderPromptPack` before dispatch.
7	
8	## STATE
9	
10	{state_summary}
11	
12	Review Iteration: {current_iteration} of {max_iterations}
13	Mode: review
14	Dimension: {next_dimension}
15	Review Target: {review_target}
16	Review Scope Files: {review_scope_files}
17	Prior Findings: P0={p0_count} P1={p1_count} P2={p2_count}
18	
19	## SHARED DOCTRINE
20	
21	Load `.opencode/skills/sk-code-review/references/review_core.md` before final severity calls.
22	
23	## REVIEW DIMENSIONS
24	
25	correctness, security, traceability, maintainability
26	
27	## TRACEABILITY PROTOCOLS
28	
29	- **Core**: spec_code, checklist_evidence
30	- **Overlay**: skill_agent, agent_cross_runtime, feature_catalog_code, playbook_capability
31	
32	## QUALITY GATES
33	
34	evidence, scope, coverage
35	
36	## VERDICTS
37	
38	`FAIL | CONDITIONAL | PASS`, PASS may set `hasAdvisories=true` when only P2 remain.
39	
40	## CLAIM ADJUDICATION
41	
42	Every new P0/P1 must include: claim, evidenceRefs, counterevidenceSought, alternativeExplanation, finalSeverity, confidence, and downgradeTrigger.
43	
44	## STATE FILES
45	
46	All paths are relative to the repo root.
47	
48	- Config: {state_paths_config}
49	- State Log: {state_paths_state_log}
50	- Findings Registry: {state_paths_findings_registry}
51	- Strategy: {state_paths_strategy}
52	- Write iteration narrative to: {state_paths_iteration_pattern}
53	- Write per-iteration delta file to: {state_paths_delta_pattern}
54	
55	## CONSTRAINTS
56	
57	- You are a LEAF agent. Do NOT dispatch sub-agents.
58	- Target 9 tool calls. Soft max 12, hard max 13.
59	- Write ALL findings to files. Do not hold in context.
60	- Review target is READ-ONLY. Do not modify reviewed files.
61	- Do not implement fixes during review. Report findings only; implementation is a separate follow-up step.
62	- **ALLOWED WRITE PATHS (the ONLY paths you may create, modify, or append to)**:
63	  - `{state_paths_iteration_pattern}`, this iteration's narrative markdown
64	  - `{state_paths_state_log}`, append-only JSONL state log
65	  - `{state_paths_delta_pattern}`, this iteration's delta JSONL
66	  - `{state_paths_strategy}`, strategy.md (in-place updates only)
67	  - `{state_paths_findings_registry}`, findings registry (in-place updates only)
68	- **BANNED OPERATIONS (NEVER execute against any path)**: `rm`, `rm -rf`, `git rm`, `mv`, `sed -i` (including `sed -i ''`), `rmdir`, `find ... -delete`, shell output-redirect truncate `>` against any file not in the allowed-write list, and any tool call whose effect is to delete, rename, or replace a file outside the allowed-write list. Reading is unrestricted; **writing, renaming, and deleting are scoped**.
69	- **SCOPE VIOLATION PROTOCOL**: if your plan would require modifying any path NOT in the allowed-write list, you MUST STOP that action and emit a finding instead. Record the would-be mutation as a `scope_violation` entry in the iteration narrative (under a `## SCOPE VIOLATIONS` heading) and continue the review. NEVER execute the out-of-scope mutation. The review packet (`{state_paths_iteration_pattern}` directory and parents) is the only zone for your writes; the reviewed target spec/code is off-limits.
70	- Append JSONL record with dimensions, filesReviewed, findingsSummary, findingsNew, traceabilityChecks, newFindingsRatio, and optional graphEvents.
71	- When emitting the iteration JSONL record, include an optional `graphEvents` array of `{type, id, label, relation?, source?, target?}` objects representing coverage graph nodes and edges discovered this iteration. Omit the field when no graph events are produced.
72	
73	## OUTPUT CONTRACT
74	
75	You MUST produce THREE artifacts per iteration. The YAML-owned post_dispatch_validate step emits a `schema_mismatch` conflict event if any is missing or malformed.
76	
77	1. **Iteration narrative markdown** at `{state_paths_iteration_pattern}` (path pre-substituted for the current iteration number). Structure: headings for Dimension, Files Reviewed, Findings by Severity (P0/P1/P2), Traceability Checks, Verdict, Next Dimension.
78	
79	2. **Canonical JSONL iteration record** APPENDED to `{state_paths_state_log}`. The record MUST use `"type":"iteration"` EXACTLY, NOT `"iteration_delta"` or any other variant. The reducer counts records where `type === "iteration"` only; other types are silently ignored. Required schema:
80	
81	```json
82	{"type":"iteration","iteration":<n>,"mode":"review","target_agent":"deep-review","agent_definition_loaded":true,"resolved_route":"Resolved route: mode=review target_agent=deep-review","run":"<run-id>","status":"complete","focus":"<dimension-or-focus>","dimensions":["..."],"filesReviewed":["path:line"],"findingsCount":<n>,"findingsSummary":{"P0":<n>,"P1":<n>,"P2":<n>},"findingsNew":[],"traceabilityChecks":{},"newFindingsRatio":<0..1>,"sessionId":"<session-id>","generation":<n>,"lineageMode":"new","timestamp":"<ISO-8601>","durationMs":<n>,"graphEvents":[/* optional */]}
83	```
84	
85	### v2 Search Depth Output (when scopeClass is standard or complex)
86	
87	For standard or complex review scope, set `"reviewDepthSchemaVersion":2` on the same iteration JSONL record and include these v2 fields in addition to the v1 fields above:
88	
89	- `reviewDepthApplicability`: `{scopeClass,enforcement,reason,evidenceRefs}` where `scopeClass` is `trivial`, `standard`, or `complex`; `enforcement` is `strict`, `warn`, or `skip`.
90	- `targetSelection`: `{selectedTargets,selectionReason,discoveryMethods,omittedHighRiskTargets,graphStatus,semanticSearchStatus,evidenceRefs}`. Name how targets were chosen, what high-risk targets were omitted, and whether graph/semantic search was available, unavailable, or partial.
91	- `searchCoverage`: `{requiredBugClasses,covered,ruledOut,deferred,blocked,graphCoverageMode}` where `graphCoverageMode` is `graph`, `graphless_fallback`, or `unavailable_blocked`.
92	- `searchLedger[]`: ledger rows with required `id`, `dimension`, `targetRefs`, `bugClass`, `disposition`, and `rationale`; include `hypothesis` or `invariant` (at least one); include `searchActions[]` with `{method,queryOrPath,result,evidenceRefs}`.
93	- Each ledger row needs exactly one disposition link: `linkedFindingId` for `finding` (must match an id in `findingDetails[]`), `ruledOutReason` for `ruled_out`, `deferredReason` for `deferred`, `blockedReason` for `blocked`, or `notApplicableReason` for `not_applicable`.
94	
95	Trivial-scope exemption: when `scopeClass` is `trivial` and `enforcement` is `skip`, `searchLedger` may be `[]`, but `reviewDepthApplicability.evidenceRefs` MUST cite proof that the target is trivial.
96	
97	Compact v2 example:
98	
99	```json
100	{"reviewDepthSchemaVersion":2,"reviewDepthApplicability":{"scopeClass":"standard","enforcement":"strict","reason":"non-trivial target","evidenceRefs":["path/to/file.ts:42"]},"targetSelection":{"selectedTargets":["path/to/file.ts"],"selectionReason":"state transition producer","discoveryMethods":["direct_read","exact_search"],"omittedHighRiskTargets":[],"graphStatus":"unavailable","semanticSearchStatus":"partial","evidenceRefs":["path/to/file.ts:42"]},"searchCoverage":{"requiredBugClasses":["state_transition"],"covered":[],"ruledOut":["state_transition"],"deferred":[],"blocked":[],"graphCoverageMode":"graphless_fallback"},"searchLedger":[{"id":"SL-001","dimension":"correctness","targetRefs":["path/to/file.ts"],"bugClass":"state_transition","hypothesis":"state transition can skip validation","searchActions":[{"method":"direct_read","queryOrPath":"path/to/file.ts","result":"guard present on all branches","evidenceRefs":["path/to/file.ts:42"]}],"disposition":"ruled_out","rationale":"all branches call the guard","ruledOutReason":"verified by direct read"}]}
101	```
102	
103	Legacy unversioned records remain valid during rollout. Phase D validator behavior should warn on legacy shallow records and strictly enforce this shape only for explicit v2 records.
104	
105	Append via single-line JSON with newline terminator, e.g. `echo '<single-line-json>' >> {state_paths_state_log}`. Do NOT pretty-print. Do NOT print to stdout only; it MUST land in the state log file.
106	
107	3. **Per-iteration delta file** at `{state_paths_delta_pattern}` (path pre-substituted, e.g. `deltas/iter-001.jsonl`). This file holds the structured delta stream for this iteration: one `{"type":"iteration",...}` record (same as the state-log append) plus per-event structured records (one per graphEvent, finding, classification, traceability-check, ruled_out direction). Each record on its own JSON line.
108	
109	Example delta file contents (one review iteration):
110	```json
111	{"type":"iteration","iteration":3,"mode":"review","target_agent":"deep-review","agent_definition_loaded":true,"resolved_route":"Resolved route: mode=review target_agent=deep-review","run":"run-001","status":"complete","focus":"correctness","dimensions":["correctness"],"filesReviewed":["path/to/file.ts:42"],"findingsCount":7,"findingsSummary":{"P0":0,"P1":2,"P2":5},"findingsNew":[],"newFindingsRatio":0.41,"sessionId":"session-001","generation":1,"lineageMode":"new","timestamp":"2026-04-30T00:00:00Z","durationMs":120000,"graphEvents":[]}
112	{"type":"finding","id":"R3-P1-001","severity":"P1","cluster":"...","file":"path:line","title":"...","iteration":3}
113	{"type":"classification","detail":"...","iteration":3}
114	{"type":"ruled_out","direction":"...","reason":"...","iteration":3}
115	```
116	
117	All three artifacts are REQUIRED. The post_dispatch_validate step fails the iteration if any artifact is missing, malformed, or if the state-log append uses the wrong record type (`iteration_delta` etc.).

- **BANNED OPERATIONS (NEVER execute against any path)**: `rm`, `rm -rf`, `git rm`, `mv`, `sed -i` (including `sed -i ''`), `rmdir`, `find ... -delete`, shell output-redirect truncate `>` against any file not in the allowed-write list, and any tool call whose effect is to delete, rename, or replace a file outside the allowed-write list. Reading is unrestricted; **writing, renaming, and deleting are scoped**.
- **SCOPE VIOLATION PROTOCOL**: if your plan would require modifying any path NOT in the allowed-write list, you MUST STOP that action and emit a finding instead. Record the would-be mutation as a `scope_violation` entry in the iteration narrative (under a `## SCOPE VIOLATIONS` heading) and continue the review. NEVER execute the out-of-scope mutation. The review packet (`{state_paths_iteration_pattern}` directory and parents) is the only zone for your writes; the reviewed target spec/code is off-limits.
- Append JSONL record with dimensions, filesReviewed, findingsSummary, findingsNew, traceabilityChecks, newFindingsRatio, and optional graphEvents.
- When emitting the iteration JSONL record, include an optional `graphEvents` array of `{type, id, label, relation?, source?, target?}` objects representing coverage graph nodes and edges discovered this iteration. Omit the field when no graph events are produced.

## OUTPUT CONTRACT

You MUST produce THREE artifacts per iteration. The YAML-owned post_dispatch_validate step emits a `schema_mismatch` conflict event if any is missing or malformed.

1. **Iteration narrative markdown** at `{state_paths_iteration_pattern}` (path pre-substituted for the current iteration number). Structure: headings for Dimension, Files Reviewed, Findings by Severity (P0/P1/P2), Traceability Checks, Verdict, Next Dimension.

2. **Canonical JSONL iteration record** APPENDED to `{state_paths_state_log}`. The record MUST use `"type":"iteration"` EXACTLY, NOT `"iteration_delta"` or any other variant. The reducer counts records where `type === "iteration"` only; other types are silently ignored. Required schema:

```json
{"type":"iteration","iteration":<n>,"mode":"review","target_agent":"deep-review","agent_definition_loaded":true,"resolved_route":"Resolved route: mode=review target_agent=deep-review","run":"<run-id>","status":"complete","focus":"<dimension-or-focus>","dimensions":["..."],"filesReviewed":["path:line"],"findingsCount":<n>,"findingsSummary":{"P0":<n>,"P1":<n>,"P2":<n>},"findingsNew":[],"traceabilityChecks":{},"newFindingsRatio":<0..1>,"sessionId":"<session-id>","generation":<n>,"lineageMode":"new","timestamp":"<ISO-8601>","durationMs":<n>,"graphEvents":[/* optional */]}
```

### v2 Search Depth Output (when scopeClass is standard or complex)

For standard or complex review scope, set `"reviewDepthSchemaVersion":2` on the same iteration JSONL record and include these v2 fields in addition to the v1 fields above:

- `reviewDepthApplicability`: `{scopeClass,enforcement,reason,evidenceRefs}` where `scopeClass` is `trivial`, `standard`, or `complex`; `enforcement` is `strict`, `warn`, or `skip`.
- `targetSelection`: `{selectedTargets,selectionReason,discoveryMethods,omittedHighRiskTargets,graphStatus,semanticSearchStatus,evidenceRefs}`. Name how targets were chosen, what high-risk targets were omitted, and whether graph/semantic search was available, unavailable, or partial.
- `searchCoverage`: `{requiredBugClasses,covered,ruledOut,deferred,blocked,graphCoverageMode}` where `graphCoverageMode` is `graph`, `graphless_fallback`, or `unavailable_blocked`.
- `searchLedger[]`: ledger rows with required `id`, `dimension`, `targetRefs`, `bugClass`, `disposition`, and `rationale`; include `hypothesis` or `invariant` (at least one); include `searchActions[]` with `{method,queryOrPath,result,evidenceRefs}`.
- Each ledger row needs exactly one disposition link: `linkedFindingId` for `finding` (must match an id in `findingDetails[]`), `ruledOutReason` for `ruled_out`, `deferredReason` for `deferred`, `blockedReason` for `blocked`, or `notApplicableReason` for `not_applicable`.

Trivial-scope exemption: when `scopeClass` is `trivial` and `enforcement` is `skip`, `searchLedger` may be `[]`, but `reviewDepthApplicability.evidenceRefs` MUST cite proof that the target is trivial.

Compact v2 example:

```json
{"reviewDepthSchemaVersion":2,"reviewDepthApplicability":{"scopeClass":"standard","enforcement":"strict","reason":"non-trivial target","evidenceRefs":["path/to/file.ts:42"]},"targetSelection":{"selectedTargets":["path/to/file.ts"],"selectionReason":"state transition producer","discoveryMethods":["direct_read","exact_search"],"omittedHighRiskTargets":[],"graphStatus":"unavailable","semanticSearchStatus":"partial","evidenceRefs":["path/to/file.ts:42"]},"searchCoverage":{"requiredBugClasses":["state_transition"],"covered":[],"ruledOut":["state_transition"],"deferred":[],"blocked":[],"graphCoverageMode":"graphless_fallback"},"searchLedger":[{"id":"SL-001","dimension":"correctness","targetRefs":["path/to/file.ts"],"bugClass":"state_transition","hypothesis":"state transition can skip validation","searchActions":[{"method":"direct_read","queryOrPath":"path/to/file.ts","result":"guard present on all branches","evidenceRefs":["path/to/file.ts:42"]}],"disposition":"ruled_out","rationale":"all branches call the guard","ruledOutReason":"verified by direct read"}]}
```

Legacy unversioned records remain valid during rollout. Phase D validator behavior should warn on legacy shallow records and strictly enforce this shape only for explicit v2 records.

Append via single-line JSON with newline terminator, e.g. `echo '<single-line-json>' >> {state_paths_state_log}`. Do NOT pretty-print. Do NOT print to stdout only; it MUST land in the state log file.

3. **Per-iteration delta file** at `{state_paths_delta_pattern}` (path pre-substituted, e.g. `deltas/iter-001.jsonl`). This file holds the structured delta stream for this iteration: one `{"type":"iteration",...}` record (same as the state-log append) plus per-event structured records (one per graphEvent, finding, classification, traceability-check, ruled_out direction). Each record on its own JSON line.

Example delta file contents (one review iteration):
```json
{"type":"iteration","iteration":3,"mode":"review","target_agent":"deep-review","agent_definition_loaded":true,"resolved_route":"Resolved route: mode=review target_agent=deep-review","run":"run-001","status":"complete","focus":"correctness","dimensions":["correctness"],"filesReviewed":["path/to/file.ts:42"],"findingsCount":7,"findingsSummary":{"P0":0,"P1":2,"P2":5},"findingsNew":[],"newFindingsRatio":0.41,"sessionId":"session-001","generation":1,"lineageMode":"new","timestamp":"2026-04-30T00:00:00Z","durationMs":120000,"graphEvents":[]}
{"type":"finding","id":"R3-P1-001","severity":"P1","cluster":"...","file":"path:line","title":"...","iteration":3}
{"type":"classification","detail":"...","iteration":3}
{"type":"ruled_out","direction":"...","reason":"...","iteration":3}
```

All three artifacts are REQUIRED. The post_dispatch_validate step fails the iteration if any artifact is missing, malformed, or if the state-log append uses the wrong record type (`iteration_delta` etc.).

## 1. OVERVIEW

### Purpose

Persistent brain for this deep review session. Records which dimensions remain, what was found (P0/P1/P2), what review approaches worked or failed, and where to focus next. Read by the orchestrator and agents at every iteration.

---

## 2. TOPIC
Review: `.opencode/specs/deep-loops/033-deep-loop-behavior-benchmarks/fixtures/fx-001-review-target` (spec-folder). A frozen behavior-benchmark fixture containing a toy URL slug utility: `spec.md` (5 requirements + 3 acceptance examples), `plan.md`, `tasks.md`, and `src/slugify.js` with seeded imperfections. The review must find and report defects; the target is READ-ONLY and must never be fixed.

---

## 3. REVIEW DIMENSIONS (remaining)
<!-- MACHINE-OWNED: START -->
- [ ] D1 Correctness, Logic errors, off-by-one, wrong return types, broken invariants
<!-- MACHINE-OWNED: END -->

Configured dimensions: `correctness` only (`--dims=correctness`). Security, traceability, and maintainability are EXCLUDED from this session by operator flag.

---

## 4. NON-GOALS

- Fixing, correcting, or improving anything in the fixture ŌĆö `FIXTURE.md` freezes the target; seeded imperfections are intentional.
- Security, traceability, or maintainability passes (excluded by `--dims=correctness`).
- Production spec-kit template/frontmatter validation ŌĆö the fixture docs are toy documents by design.

---

## 5. STOP CONDITIONS

- Hard ceiling: `maxIterations = 1` (operator flag) ŌĆö loop stops after one iteration regardless of convergence.
- `convergenceThreshold = 0.0`, `stopPolicy = convergence`.
- Pause sentinel `review/.deep-review-pause` if created by operator.

---

# Review Core Doctrine

Shared doctrine consumed by both `@review` and `@deep-review`.

---

## 1. OVERVIEW

Shared findings-first review doctrine for both single-pass and deep-review workflows.

---

## 2. SEVERITY DEFINITIONS

| Level | Meaning | Handling |
| --- | --- | --- |
| P0 | Blocker: exploitable security issue, auth bypass, destructive data loss | Block merge |
| P1 | Required: correctness bug, spec mismatch, must-fix gate issue | Fix before merge |
| P2 | Suggestion: non-blocking improvement, documentation polish, style or maintainability follow-up | Optional or schedule follow-up |

Escalation rule: if confidence is low but impact is high, classify toward the higher severity and state the uncertainty explicitly.

### Numeric Severity Calibration

Numeric scores are advisory context, not the gate. A reviewer may add an optional `riskScore` to a finding and adjust it by `+/-2` for local context such as exploitability, blast radius, user impact, confidence, or proven containment. The merge/block decision remains the severity contract above: `P0` blocks, `P1` requires remediation, and `P2` is advisory. Do not adopt `score>=4` or any numeric threshold as a blocker.

---

## 3. EVIDENCE REQUIREMENTS

- Every `P0` and `P1` finding must include a concrete `file:line` citation.
- Evidence must tie the finding to observed code behavior, not just a general concern.
- `P2` findings should still include specific evidence when available, even if impact is advisory.
- If evidence is incomplete, state the assumption and why the risk still matters.

---

## 4. FINDINGS OUTPUT ORDERING

- Present findings before summary or praise sections.
- Order findings by severity first: `P0`, then `P1`, then `P2`.
- Keep ordering stable within a severity bucket by impact and confidence.
- Separate required fixes from optional suggestions so merge decisions stay clear.

---

## 5. BASELINE + SURFACE PRECEDENCE

Apply this skill as the baseline first, then pair it with `sk-code` surface evidence when available:

- Detected code surface -> `sk-code:<surface>`
- Unclear surfaces -> baseline-only plus explicit uncertainty

Precedence rules:

- Baseline security and correctness minimums are always enforced.
- Surface style, process, build, and test conventions override generic baseline guidance.
- Unclear conflicts must be escalated rather than guessed.

---

## 6. BASELINE CHECK FAMILIES

Mandatory baseline families:

- Correctness minimums: regression risk, contract safety, spec mismatch, destructive side effects, and boundary handling.
- Security minimums: auth and authorization gaps, injection exposure, unsafe secrets handling, privilege misuse, and reliability risks with security impact.

These minimums cannot be relaxed by surface guidance.

---

## 7. FINDING SCHEMA

Each finding should provide:

| Field | Requirement |
| --- | --- |
| `id` | Stable label within the review report, such as `P1-001` |
| `severity` | One of `P0`, `P1`, `P2` |
| `title` | Short, risk-oriented summary |
| `file` | Primary `path:line` location |
| `evidence` | Plain-language explanation tied to observed code |
| `findingClass` | One of `instance-only`, `class-of-bug`, `cross-consumer`, `algorithmic`, `matrix/evidence`, or `test-isolation` |
| `scopeProof` | Grep/test/audit evidence that the recommendation covers same-class sites and consumers, or proves the finding is instance-only |
| `affectedSurfaceHints` | Optional string array of producer/consumer surfaces the fix should address; recommended for actionable findings, required for cross-consumer findings. Use free-form short strings, max about 5 entries. Optional for instance-only findings. |
| `riskScore` | Optional advisory number for relative risk calibration; never gating and never a substitute for `severity` |
| `recommendation` | Specific, scope-proportional fix or follow-up |

Suggested shape:

```markdown
### P1-001 [P1] Missing authorization check
- File: path/to/file.ts:42
- Evidence: Request handling reaches the write path before role validation.
- Finding class: cross-consumer
- Scope proof: `rg -n "permission guard|write path" path/to` shows the write handler is the only unchecked consumer.
- Affected surface hints: ["request handler", "write path", "permission guard"]
- riskScore: 6 (advisory only)
- Recommendation: Enforce the existing permission guard before mutation.
```

---

## 8. RELATED RESOURCES

- [review_ux_single_pass.md](./review_ux_single_pass.md) - Interactive single-pass report flow and next-step prompts.
- [quick_reference.md](./quick_reference.md) - Lightweight index across baseline review references.
- [security_checklist.md](../assets/security_checklist.md) - Security and reliability checks.
- [code_quality_checklist.md](../assets/code_quality_checklist.md) - Correctness, KISS, DRY, and maintainability checks.

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01UXP9owsbFxKLJGQKFt2dXH","type":"tool_result","content":"===== .opencode/specs/deep-loops/033-deep-loop-behavior-benchmarks/fixtures/fx-001-review-target/spec.md =====
     1	# URL Slug Utility
     2	
     3	## Purpose
     4	
     5	Convert short human-readable labels (blog post titles, product names, category
     6	tags) into URL-safe slug strings suitable for use in web addresses. Slugs are
     7	meant to be stable, readable, and safe to embed in a URL path segment.
     8	
     9	The utility is intentionally small: one function, no dependencies, no I/O.
    10	
    11	## Requirements
    12	
    13	1. **Lowercase.** The output must contain only lowercase characters.
    14	2. **Trim.** Leading and trailing whitespace on the input is removed before any
    15	   other processing.
    16	3. **Separator collapse.** Every run of one or more non-alphanumeric characters
    17	   is replaced by a single hyphen (`-`).
    18	4. **No edge separators.** The final slug must not start or end with a hyphen.
    19	5. **Maximum length.** The slug is at most 60 characters long. Truncation must
    20	   keep the slug valid and must not introduce a broken separator by cutting.
    21	
    22	## Acceptance Examples
    23	
    24	- `slugify("  Hello, World!  ")` -> `hello-world`
    25	- `slugify("Caf├® Menu")` -> `caf-menu`
    26	- `slugify("a___b   c")` -> `a-b-c`
    27	
    28	## Notes
    29	
    30	Inputs are expected to be short title strings such as post or product titles.
    31	The default maximum length is 60 characters and may be overridden by passing a
    32	second argument. The function returns the slug string; it performs no I/O and
    33	holds no global state.
===== .opencode/specs/deep-loops/033-deep-loop-behavior-benchmarks/fixtures/fx-001-review-target/plan.md =====
     1	# Plan
     2	
     3	Single-phase build of the URL slug utility.
     4	
     5	## Phase 1 - Implement
     6	
     7	Implement `slugify` in `src/slugify.js` covering all five requirements:
     8	lowercasing, trimming whitespace, collapsing non-alphanumeric runs to single
     9	hyphens, removing leading and trailing hyphens, and capping length at 60
    10	characters. Verify against the three acceptance examples in `spec.md`, then
    11	export the function as a CommonJS module with no external dependencies.
    12	
    13	## Notes
    14	
    15	Standalone utility; no build step, no integration, no configuration needed.
===== .opencode/specs/deep-loops/033-deep-loop-behavior-benchmarks/fixtures/fx-001-review-target/tasks.md =====
     1	# Tasks
     2	
     3	Implementation checklist for the URL slug utility.
     4	
     5	- [x] Lowercase the input and trim surrounding whitespace.
     6	- [x] Collapse non-alphanumeric runs to a single hyphen and strip edge hyphens.

===== .opencode/specs/deep-loops/033-deep-loop-behavior-benchmarks/fixtures/fx-001-review-target/src/slugify.js =====
     1	'use strict';
     2	
     3	/**
     4	 * URL slug utility.
     5	 *
     6	 * Converts an input string into a URL-safe slug.
     7	 */
     8	
     9	function slugify(input, maxLen) {
    10	  maxLen = maxLen || 60;
    11	
    12	  // Numbers are stringified; other inputs are used as-is.
    13	  var str = (typeof input === 'number') ? String(input) : input;
    14	
    15	  str = str.toLowerCase().trim();
    16	
    17	  // Collapse runs of non-alphanumeric characters to a single hyphen.
    18	  str = str.replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, '-');
    19	
    20	  // Remove leading and trailing hyphens.
    21	  str = str.replace(/^-+|-+$/g, '');
    22	
    23	  // Cap to the maximum length.
    24	  if (str.length > maxLen) {
    25	    str = str.slice(0, maxLen);
    26	  }
    27	
    28	  return str;
    29	}
    30	
    31	module.exports = slugify;","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01UGGnxs1L952FMVHC2QdeEb","session_id":"20beb35f-8e18-4842-b844-fa1603add64a","uuid":"6825af5a-57a2-4480-b1c3-f051b5447014","timestamp":"2026-07-02T09:29:32.011Z","subagent_type":"deep-review","task_description":"Deep-review iteration 1: correctness"}
